results.title: Breaking quantum limits: Error correction advances in 2025

pages.title: Quantum computing breakthroughs: A 2026 review
pages.content:
Title: Quantum computing breakthroughs: A 2026 review
Authors: J. Smith, A. Brown
Year: 2026
Publication: Nature Physics
Volume: 22
Issue: 3
Pages: 45-52

Abstract: We survey the most significant experimental and theoretical breakthroughs in quantum computing between 2025 and early 2026. The review covers superconducting qubit arrays exceeding 1000 physical qubits, topological qubit demonstrations, and algorithmic improvements that bring practical quantum advantage closer. Key milestones include error rates below the surface-code threshold on multiple platforms and large-scale entanglement distribution over metropolitan networks. We discuss implications for cryptography, drug discovery, and materials science.

pages.title: Breaking quantum limits: Error correction advances in 2025
pages.content:
Title: Breaking quantum limits: Error correction advances in 2025
Authors: M. Johnson
Year: 2025
Publication: Physical Review Letters
Volume: 135
Issue: 7
Pages: 012345

Abstract: We introduce a bosonic-cat-code architecture combined with a high-efficiency readout scheme that extends the coherence time of stored logical qubits by an order of magnitude. The system operates at millikelvin temperatures and achieves a logical error rate of 2.4e-4 per cycle, outperforming previous surface-code implementations on the same hardware footprint. Detailed noise modeling and a full-cycle characterization verify the error budget, demonstrating a practical path toward fault-tolerant quantum memory. These results represent a crucial step toward scalable, error-corrected quantum computation.
